input.title: Membership and Association Management Portal
input.description: Membership and Association Management Portal is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

scope: Member Registration and Profile Management workflows
constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with Next.js and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Node.js, PostgreSQL, Stripe for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Member Registration and Profile Management
requirements: Users must be able to complete member registration and profile management through the approved entry points provided by the platform.
The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing member registration and profile management to continue.
Member Registration and Profile Management must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
The user interface for member registration and profile management should provide clear success, failure, and recovery messages for common scenarios.

FR-02.title: Membership Tier Configuration
requirements: The system must support membership tier configuration as a complete business workflow within the application.
Membership Tier Configuration should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with membership tier configuration from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to membership tier configuration for operational support.

FR-03.title: Application Review and Approval
requirements: Authorized users must be able to create, update, and complete application review and approval according to the approved business workflow.
The system should validate required fields, status transitions, and role-based actions before saving application review and approval changes.
Application Review and Approval must maintain a clear history of assignments, approvals, or status updates for operational traceability.
The interface should surface the current workflow state, next available actions, and blocking conditions for application review and approval.

FR-04.title: Renewal Reminder Automation
requirements: The system must trigger renewal reminder automation when the relevant business events or threshold conditions occur.
Users should be able to configure delivery channels, timing, or opt-in preferences related to renewal reminder automation.
Renewal Reminder Automation must record delivery outcomes, retries, and failures for operational follow-up.
The interface should let authorized users review recent renewal reminder automation events and the current notification status.

FR-05.title: Online Membership Payment
requirements: The system must provide end-to-end online membership payment capabilities for creating, updating, viewing, and retiring relevant business records.
Online Membership Payment should support role-based permissions, validation rules, and duplicate-prevention checks where appropriate.
Users must be able to search, filter, and review historical activity related to online membership payment without leaving the module context.
The platform should maintain auditability for important actions performed within online membership payment and retain operational history for reporting.

FR-06.title: Corporate Membership Accounts
requirements: Users must be able to complete corporate membership accounts through the approved entry points provided by the platform.
The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing corporate membership accounts to continue.
Corporate Membership Accounts must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
The user interface for corporate membership accounts should provide clear success, failure, and recovery messages for common scenarios.

FR-07.title: Member Directory Search
requirements: Users must be able to use member directory search to locate records quickly using relevant keywords, categories, or structured filters.
Member Directory Search should support sorting, pagination, and clear empty-state behavior when no matching records are found.
The system must return results that reflect current access permissions and only expose data the requesting user is allowed to view.
Recent selections, saved filters, or contextual shortcuts should be supported where they improve repeated use of member directory search.

FR-08.title: Committee and Chapter Management
requirements: The system must provide end-to-end committee and chapter management capabilities for creating, updating, viewing, and retiring relevant business records.
Committee and Chapter Management should support role-based permissions, validation rules, and duplicate-prevention checks where appropriate.
Users must be able to search, filter, and review historical activity related to committee and chapter management without leaving the module context.
The platform should maintain auditability for important actions performed within committee and chapter management and retain operational history for reporting.

FR-09.title: Event Registration for Members
requirements: Users must be able to complete event registration for members through the approved entry points provided by the platform.
The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing event registration for members to continue.
Event Registration for Members must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
The user interface for event registration for members should provide clear success, failure, and recovery messages for common scenarios.

FR-10.title: Continuing Education Credit Tracking
requirements: The system must capture continuing education credit tracking data in real time or near real time as operational events occur.
Continuing Education Credit Tracking should provide a timeline, status history, or change log so users can understand what happened and when.
Users must be able to review, filter, and reconcile continuing education credit tracking records against the related business entities.
The platform should flag missing, inconsistent, or exception conditions that require attention during continuing education credit tracking.

FR-11.title: Certificate Issuance
requirements: The system must support certificate issuance as a complete business workflow within the application.
Certificate Issuance should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with certificate issuance from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to certificate issuance for operational support.

FR-12.title: Document Library Access Control
requirements: Users must be able to complete document library access control through the approved entry points provided by the platform.
The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing document library access control to continue.
Document Library Access Control must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
The user interface for document library access control should provide clear success, failure, and recovery messages for common scenarios.

FR-13.title: Announcement and Newsletter Publishing
requirements: The system must support announcement and newsletter publishing as a complete business workflow within the application.
Announcement and Newsletter Publishing should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with announcement and newsletter publishing from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to announcement and newsletter publishing for operational support.

FR-14.title: Volunteer Signup Workflow
requirements: Authorized users must be able to create, update, and complete volunteer signup workflow according to the approved business workflow.
The system should validate required fields, status transitions, and role-based actions before saving volunteer signup workflow changes.
Volunteer Signup Workflow must maintain a clear history of assignments, approvals, or status updates for operational traceability.
The interface should surface the current workflow state, next available actions, and blocking conditions for volunteer signup workflow.

FR-15.title: Member Support Ticketing
requirements: Authorized users must be able to create, update, and complete member support ticketing according to the approved business workflow.
The system should validate required fields, status transitions, and role-based actions before saving member support ticketing changes.
Member Support Ticketing must maintain a clear history of assignments, approvals, or status updates for operational traceability.
The interface should surface the current workflow state, next available actions, and blocking conditions for member support ticketing.

FR-16.title: Benefits Redemption Tracking
requirements: The system must capture benefits redemption tracking data in real time or near real time as operational events occur.
Benefits Redemption Tracking should provide a timeline, status history, or change log so users can understand what happened and when.
Users must be able to review, filter, and reconcile benefits redemption tracking records against the related business entities.
The platform should flag missing, inconsistent, or exception conditions that require attention during benefits redemption tracking.

FR-17.title: Sponsor Management
requirements: The system must provide end-to-end sponsor management capabilities for creating, updating, viewing, and retiring relevant business records.
Sponsor Management should support role-based permissions, validation rules, and duplicate-prevention checks where appropriate.
Users must be able to search, filter, and review historical activity related to sponsor management without leaving the module context.
The platform should maintain auditability for important actions performed within sponsor management and retain operational history for reporting.

FR-18.title: Board Reporting Dashboard
requirements: The system must provide board reporting dashboard with role-appropriate filters, date ranges, and drill-down views.
Board Reporting Dashboard should calculate metrics from current transactional data and keep summaries aligned with source records.
Authorized users must be able to export or share board reporting dashboard outputs in formats used by business teams.
The platform should retain query history, report configuration, or dashboard preferences where that improves repeated analysis.

FR-01.title: Design Member Registration and Profile Management UI
FR-01.description: Design the user interface for member registration and profile management. Create the screens, forms, dashboards, or status views needed by the SRS and make the workflow clear, responsive, and accessible for the target users.
acceptance_criteria: UI design for member registration and profile management is complete and reviewable.
The member registration and profile management design is responsive and accessible.
The design for member registration and profile management aligns with the documented user flow and validation states.

FR-01.title: Frontend Implementation of Member Registration and Profile Management
FR-01.description: Implement the frontend behavior for member registration and profile management. Build the required user interactions, validation states, loading states, and error handling so the interface supports these behaviors: Users must be able to complete member registration and profile management through the approved entry points provided by the platform; The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing member registration and profile management to continue; Member Registration and Profile Management must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend implements the required member registration and profile management user interactions.
Validation, loading, and error states for member registration and profile management are handled in the UI.
Frontend behavior for member registration and profile management is integrated correctly with backend APIs.

FR-01.title: Backend Implementation of Member Registration and Profile Management
FR-01.description: Implement the backend logic for member registration and profile management. Build the required APIs, business rules, validation, permissions, persistence, and processing needed to support these behaviors: Users must be able to complete member registration and profile management through the approved entry points provided by the platform; The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing member registration and profile management to continue; Member Registration and Profile Management must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
acceptance_criteria: Backend logic for member registration and profile management is implemented and testable.
APIs and business rules for member registration and profile management enforce validation and security requirements.
Data handling for member registration and profile management is reliable and supports the required workflow.

FR-01.title: Database Design for Member Registration and Profile Management
FR-01.description: Design the data model and persistence strategy for member registration and profile management. Define entities, relationships, indexes, retention rules, and migration changes needed to store and query the workflow reliably.
acceptance_criteria: The data model for member registration and profile management supports the required workflow.
Relationships, constraints, and indexes for member registration and profile management are defined correctly.
Schema changes for member registration and profile management can be applied safely in target environments.

FR-01.title: Security Hardening for Member Registration and Profile Management
FR-01.description: Implement the security controls required for member registration and profile management. Cover authentication, authorization, encryption, auditing, abuse prevention, and compliance checks where the SRS or workflow calls for them.
acceptance_criteria: Security controls for member registration and profile management enforce the required authentication, authorization, or data-protection rules.
Sensitive operations and failure scenarios for member registration and profile management are logged, validated, and protected against misuse.
The implementation of member registration and profile management satisfies the relevant privacy, audit, or compliance expectations in the SRS.

FR-01.title: Testing Member Registration and Profile Management Functionality
FR-01.description: Create and execute tests for member registration and profile management. Cover the main success flows, failure scenarios, permission checks, validation rules, integrations, and operational edge cases described in the SRS so the implementation is safe to release.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the main success and failure scenarios for member registration and profile management.
Security, validation, and permission checks for member registration and profile management are verified.
Regression coverage exists for the most important member registration and profile management behaviors.

Build the required user interactions, validation states, loading states, and error handling so the interface supports these behaviors: The system must provide board reporting dashboard with role-appropriate filters, date ranges, and drill-down views; Board Reporting Dashboard should calculate metrics from current transactional data and keep summaries aligned with source records; Authorized users must be able to export or share board reporting dashboard outputs in formats used by business teams.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend implements the required board reporting dashboard user interactions.
Validation, loading, and error states for board reporting dashboard are handled in the UI.
Frontend behavior for board reporting dashboard is integrated correctly with backend APIs.

FR-18.title: Backend Implementation of Board Reporting Dashboard
FR-18.description: Implement the backend logic for board reporting dashboard. Build the required APIs, business rules, validation, permissions, persistence, and processing needed to support these behaviors: The system must provide board reporting dashboard with role-appropriate filters, date ranges, and drill-down views; Board Reporting Dashboard should calculate metrics from current transactional data and keep summaries aligned with source records; Authorized users must be able to export or share board reporting dashboard outputs in formats used by business teams.
acceptance_criteria: Backend logic for board reporting dashboard is implemented and testable.
APIs and business rules for board reporting dashboard enforce validation and security requirements.
Data handling for board reporting dashboard is reliable and supports the required workflow.

FR-18.title: Database Design for Board Reporting Dashboard
FR-18.description: Design the data model and persistence strategy for board reporting dashboard. Define entities, relationships, indexes, retention rules, and migration changes needed to store and query the workflow reliably.
acceptance_criteria: The data model for board reporting dashboard supports the required workflow.
Relationships, constraints, and indexes for board reporting dashboard are defined correctly.
Schema changes for board reporting dashboard can be applied safely in target environments.

FR-18.title: Integrate Board Reporting Dashboard with External Services
FR-18.description: Integrate board reporting dashboard with the external services or providers referenced by the SRS. Implement secure service connections, payload validation, error handling, and recovery behavior for any third-party dependency involved in the workflow.
acceptance_criteria: External or cross-system integrations required by board reporting dashboard exchange the expected payloads successfully.
Retry, timeout, and failure handling for board reporting dashboard integrations are implemented and observable.
Credentials, tokens, and outbound calls used by board reporting dashboard are configured and secured correctly.

FR-18.title: Testing Board Reporting Dashboard Functionality
FR-18.description: Create and execute tests for board reporting dashboard. Cover the main success flows, failure scenarios, permission checks, validation rules, integrations, and operational edge cases described in the SRS so the implementation is safe to release.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the main success and failure scenarios for board reporting dashboard.
Security, validation, and permission checks for board reporting dashboard are verified.
Regression coverage exists for the most important board reporting dashboard behaviors.
